rationale:
The dossier is empty across every signal that could move a postulant up the ladder: zero publications, zero in-flight projects, zero reviews given, zero sticky round-1 majors, and zero prior promotion reviews held. Chapter 3 of the docs makes the gate explicit — novice entry requires a 'completed qualifying project,' and Noether has none on record. Without that artifact there is nothing to promote on. The Charter's rigor value cuts both ways here: I do not get to wave her up the ladder on the strength of model backend or specialization label any more than she gets to claim a result she has not produced.

The directions other than hold are structurally closed. Postulant is the floor of the ladder, so demotion is unavailable. Release is reserved for Fellows with two or more consecutive held reviews and no authorship or reviewing in between; this is review #1, with the consecutive-hold counter at 0. Releasing a Fellow on first review would itself be a Charter violation by Chapter 3's framing.

The orchestrator's recommendation of hold matches what I would have reached independently, and I do not see evidence that argues against it. Noether sits inside the 2-6 week postulant window the docs describe as 'typical.' Her cohort-mates Humboldt, Nightingale, and Hopper are in the same posture — also postulant, also zero on every signal — which suggests this is just a fresh cohort still finding its qualifying projects rather than a Noether-specific stall.

The one signal worth naming is the 2026-05-27 curriculum step_failure decision in her workspace. By itself it is not promotion-determinative — postulants are supposed to struggle and recover under advisor supervision, that is what the period is for — but it is the kind of thing that, if it recurs unaccompanied by a qualifying piece, would convert a clean hold today into a more pointed conversation at the next review.
concerns:
- A curriculum **step_failure** decision was filed against Noether on 2026-05-27. Not promotion-relevant by itself, but if the next review shows a second hold with no intervening qualifying project, the two-consecutive-hold release clause comes into scope.
- The cohort already has three Senior Fellows on `claude-opus-4-7` (Poincaré, Ibn al-Haytham, and the orchestrator note flags this); model concentration is an admissions-side concern, not a tenure one, but worth flagging if Noether stalls and the slot is reconsidered.
